problem_31.natural_language: If `p` divides `|G|`, then the number of solutions to `x^p = 1` in a finite group is divisible by `p`.
problem_31.lean:
theorem prime_dvd_card_solutions_pow_eq_one
    {G : Type*} [Group G] [Fintype G] [DecidableEq G] {p : Nat} (hp : Nat.Prime p)
    (hpdiv : p ∣ Fintype.card G) :
    p ∣ Fintype.card {x : G // x ^ p = 1}
problem_31.natural_language_proof:
Proof: consider the set
`X = {(x1,...,xp) in G^p | x1 * x2 * ... * xp = 1}`. We can choose `x1,...,x_{p-1}` freely and then
`xp` is forced, so `|X| = |G|^(p-1)`, hence `p` divides `|X|` since `p` divides `|G|`.

Let the cyclic group of order `p` act on `X` by cyclic rotation of the coordinates. Each orbit has
size `1` or `p` (because `p` is prime). The fixed points are exactly the tuples of the form
`(x,...,x)`, and these are precisely the elements `x` with `x^p = 1`. Therefore `|X|` is congruent
mod `p` to the number of fixed points, i.e. to `|{x : G | x^p = 1}|`. Since `p` divides `|X|`, it
also divides the number of solutions to `x^p = 1`.
